titleStmt.title: General Marshall to Lieutenant General Albert C. Wedemeyer

head.hi: General Marshall to Lieutenant General Albert C. Wedemeyer

div.p: 81922. Mr. Byrnes anticipates necessity for making statement in New York on Tuesday or Wednesday at meeting of Security CouncilOf the United Nations. regarding reduction of American Command in China. This apropos of recent Soviet statement of withdrawal of Soviet troops from Manchuria by 15 April.See telegram No. 552, March 23, 4 p.m., from the Counselor of Embassy in China, vol. ix, p. 595. Also apropos of Mr. Byrnes statements regarding presence of American troops in China which he made in Moscow.Department of State Bulletin, December 30, 1945, pp. 1031, 1036.

Please rush reply to me as to recent status so far as your side of the matter pertains to close out of China theater by 1 May and commencement
of reduction of Marines by regiments on 1 April. I am taking this up with HullLt. Gen. John E. Hull, Assistant Chief of Staff, Operations Division, War Department. here to find out complications on this side.
It might be well for you to propose exact wording of statement by Mr. Byrnes. In the event that your Chief of Staff receives this in your absence, he must nevertheless give me reply without delay.
